meaning: adj.宽大的,仁慈的,慈悲为怀的
examples: The judge was far too lenient with him.
There is little evidence that harsher punishments deter any better than more lenient ones.
The judge was lenient with the wrongdoer.
Lenient labor policies might scare shareholders of some firms, but they work at Nucor.
The forgiveness of debts PEOPLE WHO DESCRIBE THEMSELVES as consumer advocates demand lenient bankruptcy laws.
